description: Deed of acknowledgment (iqrār). Dated: Thursday, 1 Iyyar 1418/April 1107. Yosef b. Yiṣḥaq ha-Dayyan acknowledges that he will repay the parnas Abū l-ʿAlāʾ Ṣāʿid al-Dimashqī aka ʿUlla b. Yosef ha-Levi 10 dinars over a period of 3 months. It notes that repayment is to be entered on verso, though in fact verso is blank. The document uses some of the same language as Arabic-script iqrārāt (acknowledgments), e.g., the debtor says that he acknowledges the debt, אנני מקר, followed by a standard Arabic volition clause. However as with other deeds of acknowledgment from the Jewish courts, the text is structured like a testimony, beginning with the Aramaic שהדותא. MR/ASE
description_he: שטר הודאה, 1 אייר 1107. יוסף בן יצחק הדיין מתחייב להחזיר 10 דינרים ב-3 חודשים לפרנס עוּלא בן יוסף הלוי.
